title: Infinitesimal Odds
wikitext:
[]: Infinitesimal Odds             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Sat Jul 05, 2014 12:04 pm I'm reading the Eva Commentary site. http://www.evacommentary.org/episode-01/episode-01B-scene5.html "This dialog, which makes a big deal about the infinitesimal likelihood of EVA-01's activation, is the first instance of an ongoing cliché of NGE: Namely, its habit of setting up overwhelming odds against the success of a plan (usually in connection with Misato's schemes to defeat the Angels), but then have it always work out anyway." Yeah, what IS up with that? Something to do with the dead sea scrolls? Perhaps even *gasp* a flaw in the writing? edit: Holy crap, this site only covers the first few episodes. I am heartbroken. I knew it was too good to be true!

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jul 05, 2014 12:47 pm It's a work in progress. The full series should be covered eventually.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Sat Jul 05, 2014 9:25 pm But the last update was in 2007. That's nearly a decade ago.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jul 05, 2014 9:42 pm huh? That can't be right. Episode 6 was discussed not too long ago, and 7 is in progress currently... Ah, you're looking at the old home. It's now on the wiki here .

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Sat Jul 05, 2014 9:54 pm Ah, that explains that. Glad to know they're making progress. Now, about the main point of this topic, is there any reason for the recurring "the odds get defied" thing?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Jul 05, 2014 10:09 pm It's one of the oldest sci-fi tropes. Ever.

[]:             Quote Post by Firetrout » Sat Jul 05, 2014 10:12 pm To build up drama. But everyone knows it'll work because they have to advance the plot without killing everyone, yet.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Sun Jul 06, 2014 1:01 am I think Ritsuko said they referred to it as the "Oh-Nine System" or something.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Sun Jul 06, 2014 4:38 am Awww. I was really hoping for something deep here. Well, uhhh, my head-canon is that this was all written in the dead sea scrolls, which foretell of the impossible happening. Bam.

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Sun Jul 06, 2014 7:00 am Fun fact: one of the worst moments in the English dub is when Ritsuko brings up the "0-9 System" and says its like "oni, or a Japanese devil." I wonder who thought that was a good idea.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jul 06, 2014 9:35 am Another fun fact: if you go through the show and keep track, you'll find that, when Ritsuko says "Impossible!", she's usually denying the reality in front of her eyes. By contrast, when Misato says "Impossible!" she's usually saying "that's not the way things are supposed to work, there's something fishy going on here." Put another way, Ritsuko doesn't really understand what's going on. She thinks she does, but in reality she's as clueless as everyone else. That's why Misato's "insane" plans tend to work while Ritsuko tends to be left standing around babbling about why things are impossible.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Mon Jul 07, 2014 5:41 am Bagheera wrote: Another fun fact: if you go through the show and keep track, you'll find that, when Ritsuko says "Impossible!", she's usually denying the reality in front of her eyes. By contrast, when Misato says "Impossible!" she's usually saying "that's not the way things are supposed to work, there's something fishy going on here." Put another way, Ritsuko doesn't really understand what's going on. She thinks she does, but in reality she's as clueless as everyone else. That's why Misato's "insane" plans tend to work while Ritsuko tends to be left standing around babbling about why things are impossible.
